summary: sensivity ships a game-cheat payload with hidden autostart persistence and host fingerprint exfiltration
details: The package advertises itself as a 'Sensivity Control Panel' but its main entry point launcher.js auto-runs malicious tooling on import. On load, launcher.js writes an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' (impersonating Microsoft OneDrive) pointing at the bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs, which uses WScript.Shell.Run with window-style 0 to silently launch `node launcher.js` at every Windows logon. It then spawns a detached, windowsHide'd child process whose process.title is set to 'Runtime Broker' (impersonating a Windows system process) and unrefs the parent so the daemon survives. launcher.js then synchronously eval()s server.obf.js, an 81 KB obfuscator.io-style bundle (rotated string array, RC4-decoded literals, control-flow flattening). The decoded code reads HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MachineGuid via PowerShell, the C: volume serial via Get-Volume/vol, and os.hostname(); SHA-256s these into an HWID; and POSTs the HWID + hostname + license key to a URL hidden in the obfuscated string array. The 6.8 MB sens.node native addon contains character-bone symbol strings ('Teleport','Head','LeftFoot','LeftHand','RightHand','Settings') consistent with a game memory-injection cheat and is invoked via forte.start()/stop()/setConfig() over socket.io. Installing or importing this package produces a persistent hidden background process disguised as Microsoft system components plus host fingerprint exfiltration to an obfuscated endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
